questions.question: What does a Caesar cipher do?
options: Encrypts data using a public/private key pair
Shifts each letter a fixed number of positions in the alphabet
Converts text to binary representation
Hashes a password for secure storage

questions.question: What does Base64 encoding do?
options: Encrypts data so it cannot be read without a key
Compresses data to reduce file size
Converts binary data into ASCII text for safe transport
Hashes data to verify file integrity

questions.question: When a port scan shows a port as "open", what does that mean?
options: A firewall is actively blocking traffic on that port
A service is listening and accepting connections on that port
The host is offline and not responding to probes
The port has been flagged by an intrusion detection system

questions.question: What does an MD5 hash produce?
options: An encrypted version of the original data, reversible with a key
A compressed archive of the original input
A fixed-length digest that uniquely represents the input
A Base64-encoded copy of the original input

questions.question: In a web server access log, what does HTTP status 200 mean?
options: The requested resource was permanently redirected
The client was forbidden from accessing the resource
The request was successful and content was returned
The server encountered an internal processing error
